title: Implement a stack using linked list.
description: Implement a stack using a singly linked list.

title: Implement a graph with adjacency list.
description: Implement an undirected graph using an adjacency list.

title: Find the diameter of a binary tree.
description: Find the length of the longest path between any two nodes in a binary tree.

title: Check if binary tree is symmetric.
description: Check if a binary tree is a mirror of itself.

title: Implement quicksort.
description: Implement the quicksort algorithm.

title: What is memoization?
description: What is memoization and when is it useful?
options: Caching the results of expensive function calls and returning the cached result for the same inputs; key optimization for recursive DP problems
correct_answer: Caching the results of expensive function calls and returning the cached result for the same inputs; key optimization for recursive DP problems

title: What is the time complexity of hash table operations?
description: Average and worst case for hash tables.
options: Average: O(1) for insert/lookup/delete; Worst: O(n) due to hash collisions causing all keys to land in the same bucket
correct_answer: Average: O(1) for insert/lookup/delete; Worst: O(n) due to hash collisions causing all keys to land in the same bucket

title: What is the difference between stable and unstable sorting?
description: What makes a sort stable?
options: A stable sort preserves the relative order of equal elements; merge sort, insertion sort, TimSort are stable; quicksort, heapsort are unstable
correct_answer: A stable sort preserves the relative order of equal elements; merge sort, insertion sort, TimSort are stable; quicksort, heapsort are unstable

title: What is a B-tree?
description: What is a B-tree used for?
options: A self-balancing tree with multiple keys per node and more than two children; optimized for disk read/write — the basis for database indexes
correct_answer: A self-balancing tree with multiple keys per node and more than two children; optimized for disk read/write — the basis for database indexes

title: What is the difference between adjacency list and adjacency matrix?
description: When to prefer one over the other?
options: List: O(V+E) space, efficient for sparse graphs, O(degree) edge check. Matrix: O(V²) space, O(1) edge check, better for dense graphs
correct_answer: List: O(V+E) space, efficient for sparse graphs, O(degree) edge check. Matrix: O(V²) space, O(1) edge check, better for dense graphs

title: Container with most water.
description: Given heights, find two lines that form a container with the most water.

title: Find minimum in rotated sorted array.
description: Find the minimum element in a sorted array that has been rotated.

title: Search in rotated sorted array.
description: Search for a target in a rotated sorted array in O(log n).

title: Valid sudoku.
description: Determine if a 9x9 Sudoku board is valid (only check filled cells).

title: Flatten a nested array.
description: Flatten an arbitrarily nested array without using flat().

title: Find the single number.
description: Given an array where every element appears twice except one, find that single element.

title: What is Kruskal's algorithm?
description: What does Kruskal's algorithm find?
options: Finds the Minimum Spanning Tree of a weighted undirected graph by sorting edges by weight and adding them if they don't form a cycle (using Union-Find)
correct_answer: Finds the Minimum Spanning Tree of a weighted undirected graph by sorting edges by weight and adding them if they don't form a cycle (using Union-Find)

title: What is the Floyd-Warshall algorithm?
description: What does Floyd-Warshall compute?
options: All-pairs shortest paths in a weighted graph (including negative edges); O(V³) time using dynamic programming; detects negative cycles
correct_answer: All-pairs shortest paths in a weighted graph (including negative edges); O(V³) time using dynamic programming; detects negative cycles

title: Implement a basic hash map.
description: Implement a simple hash map with put, get, and remove.

title: What is counting sort?
description: How does counting sort work?
options: A non-comparison sort for integers in a known range; counts occurrences of each value, then places them; O(n+k) time where k is the range — linear but limited to discrete values
correct_answer: A non-comparison sort for integers in a known range; counts occurrences of each value, then places them; O(n+k) time where k is the range — linear but limited to discrete values

title: Subsets (power set).
description: Generate all possible subsets of a given array.

title: N-Queens problem.
description: Place N queens on an NxN chessboard so no two queens threaten each other.

title: Maximum path sum in binary tree.
description: Find the maximum path sum in a binary tree (path can start and end at any node).

title: What is the A* search algorithm?
description: What makes A* different from Dijkstra?
options: A* uses a heuristic function h(n) in addition to the actual cost g(n) to guide search; f(n) = g(n) + h(n). With an admissible heuristic, A* is optimal and usually faster than Dijkstra
correct_answer: A* uses a heuristic function h(n) in addition to the actual cost g(n) to guide search; f(n) = g(n) + h(n). With an admissible heuristic, A* is optimal and usually faster than Dijkstra

title: Daily temperatures.
description: Given daily temperatures, find for each day how many days you need to wait for a warmer day.

title: LCA in a binary tree (not BST).
description: Find the lowest common ancestor of two nodes in a general binary tree.

title: Course schedule (cycle detection).
description: Given prerequisites, determine if all courses can be finished (detect cycles in directed graph).

title: What is Bellman-Ford algorithm?
description: What does Bellman-Ford do differently from Dijkstra?
options: Finds shortest paths from a single source and handles negative edge weights (Dijkstra cannot); O(VE) time; can detect negative cycles
correct_answer: Finds shortest paths from a single source and handles negative edge weights (Dijkstra cannot); O(VE) time; can detect negative cycles

title: What is the difference between DFS and BFS for shortest path?
description: When should you use BFS vs DFS for shortest path?
options: BFS guarantees shortest path in unweighted graphs (explores level by level); DFS does not guarantee shortest path. For weighted graphs, use Dijkstra or Bellman-Ford
correct_answer: BFS guarantees shortest path in unweighted graphs (explores level by level); DFS does not guarantee shortest path. For weighted graphs, use Dijkstra or Bellman-Ford
